title: 39 By BP
description: This dataset comprises neurophysiological recordings from 39 participants who received calibrated laser stimuli while rating pain intensity and unpleasantness. Participants underwent a standardized pain assessment protocol involving laser stimulation at individually-determined energy levels, followed by subjective pain ratings on a 0-10 numeric rating scale. The dataset supports investigation of pain perception mechanisms and the relationship between nociceptive stimulation and subjective pain experience.
methods_description: Laser stimuli of varying intensities were administered to participants. Individual pain thresholds were determined by identifying the energy intensity corresponding to an average pain rating of 7. Each participant received 10 laser stimulations and rated pain intensity and unpleasantness on a numeric rating scale (0-10) via mouse-click responses on a screen. Participants were instructed to maintain visual fixation on a crosshair, with 5-second intervals between stimulation and rating, and randomized inter-trial intervals of 5 seconds following pain ratings.
